meta.title: MS-FAE-2: Fabric Modeling, DirectLake, and Delivery Patterns

domains.name: SQL Analytics Endpoint and Lakehouse SQL

questions.stem: A candidate is reviewing certification objectives. which Microsoft Fabric feature provides a single, tenant-wide logical data lake for Fabric workloads?
questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because Data Warehouse does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**B** is correct because OneLake matches the documented behavior or feature used in this scenario.

**C** is incorrect because Dataflows Gen2 does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**D** is incorrect because Deployment pipelines does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

See [Official reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-overview).

questions.stem: A certification-style scenario describes how team wants to expose files stored in Amazon S3 inside Fabric without copying them into OneLake first. Which feature should they use?
questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because Using a DirectLake semantic model does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**B** is correct because Using a OneLake shortcut matches the documented behavior or feature used in this scenario.

**C** is incorrect because Using a deployment pipeline rule does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**D** is incorrect because Using a KQL database does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

See [Official reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-overview).

questions.stem: A team is evaluating the next step. what is the main difference between a Fabric Lakehouse and a Fabric Data Warehouse?

options.text: Lakehouses support only CSV files while warehouses support Delta tables
Lakehouses are Spark-first over Delta files, whereas warehouses provide a SQL data warehousing experience
Lakehouses do not create a SQL endpoint

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because Lakehouses support only CSV files while warehouses support Delta tables does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**B** is correct because Lakehouses are Spark-first over Delta files, whereas warehouses provide a SQL data warehousing experience matches the documented behavior or feature used in this scenario.

**C** is incorrect because Warehouses cannot enforce ACID transactions does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**D** is incorrect because Lakehouses do not create a SQL endpoint does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

See [Official reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview).

questions.stem: A team is evaluating the next step. what is the SQL analytics endpoint of a Fabric Lakehouse primarily used for?

**B** is incorrect because Executing Spark notebooks with Python does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**C** is incorrect because Configuring workspace capacity settings does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

**D** is incorrect because Publishing Power BI apps does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.

See [Official reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview).

questions.stem: A candidate is reviewing certification objectives. which behavior should an analytics engineer expect when a DirectLake semantic model cannot stay in DirectLake mode for a query?

questions.stem: A candidate is reviewing certification objectives. which statement about lakehouse maintenance is accurate in Fabric?

options.text: Engineers can run optimization and cleanup tasks such as `OPTIMIZE` and `VACUUM` on Delta tables

Maintenance requires converting the lakehouse to a warehouse

questions.explanation:

**B** is incorrect because Lakehouses do not support table maintenance does not best fit the documented requirement in this scenario.
